title: Tapping for Daily Wealth Habits
subtitle: A tapping practice for building consistent daily money habits
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 50 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the on-and-off pattern with money check-ins and the pull toward avoidance, and reframes daily money care as something that can feel steady and light.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script for this practice is adapted from Day 50 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you've been skipping your daily money check-in or keep starting and stopping with tracking.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 50 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the start-and-stop pattern with money habits: the check-ins started, the tracking dropped, the review planned and avoided. The reframe moves toward consistent daily care that feels easy rather than effortful.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I haven't always stuck with money habits, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I've started and stopped before, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I worry I'll slip again, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I've skipped regular money check-ins.
Side of eye: I've been on and off with budgets.
Under eye: I sometimes avoid reviewing numbers.
Under nose: I forget to track what's growing.
Chin: I've put off simple daily steps.
Collarbone: I want consistency without pressure.
Under arm: I'm ready to make good habits easy.
Top of head: I'm ready to build steady wealth habits.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I enjoy simple daily actions for wealth.
Side of eye: I track money with calm and ease.
Under eye: I love seeing progress every day.
Under nose: I stay steady without needing perfection.
Chin: I trust these small steps to create big results.
Collarbone: I celebrate consistency over hustle.
Under arm: I feel proud of my daily money care.
Top of head: I lock in habits that grow my wealth.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 50 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
